name: Young plasma AD pilot was feasible with limited efficacy signal
summary: The PLASMA pilot supported feasibility and tolerability of young plasma infusions in Alzheimer disease but did not establish clear cognitive efficacy.
statement: Young plasma infusions in a very small Alzheimer disease pilot were feasible and generally tolerable, with exploratory functional signals but no clear cognitive efficacy signal suitable for a broad aging claim.
time_horizon: Four weekly infusions in randomized crossover and open-label components
quantitative_note: The staged baseline uses this as human feasibility and safety context, not as proof of efficacy.
caveats: The study was not powered to establish clinical efficacy.
Alzheimer disease context does not establish broad healthy-aging benefit.
